synthesis.summary: Toast is being added to the S&P MidCap 400 index, which mechanically forces index funds and ETFs tracking that benchmark to buy TOST shares, driving the ~7% surge. Volume is below average (rel_volume 0.72), suggesting the move is driven more by forced index-rebalancing demand than broad market participation.

ORCL is down 4.4% today and 16.1% over the past five days, but none of the supplied news addresses the company directly — the only item is a QQQ-vs-Bitcoin ETF comparison piece. The move, which is occurring on well-below-average volume (rel_volume 0.52), is not explained by any available news; it may reflect post-earnings or macro-driven sector rotation, but that is not supported by the inputs here.

RKLB is down 8% today with no high-impact news to explain the move. The only relevant news item is a low-impact technical analysis piece reacting to the drop itself, not causing it. The 5-day loss of 16% and RSI near oversold territory suggest sustained selling pressure, likely from profit-taking, sector rotation, or options flow that isn't captured in the available news.

INTC is up 1.58% today on very low relative volume (0.18x), which suggests this is a continuation of the strong 5-day trend (+14.76%) rather than a news-driven move. The only notable news — Nancy Pelosi's disclosed purchase of up to $6M in Intel stock — is classified as low-impact and likely contributed some sentiment tailwind, but a congressional disclosure alone doesn't explain the magnitude of the broader weekly move or today's specific action.
